mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the effective federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of observations when the rate is higher than one year prior.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, it signals deteriorating household income expectations and a downward revision in consumption-driven corporate revenues, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC of UNRATE momentum over a 3-year rolling window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated levels of the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate restrictive real monetary conditions that simultaneously compress valuation multiples and squeeze profit margins, and their joint deviation from long-run trend predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the effect of either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not add significant explanatory power beyond main effects in a nested regression predicting 20-day returns (F-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, stronger-than-trend retail sales signal persistent demand pressures that compel the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, causing equity risk premiums to expand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow strong RSAFS readings in more than 55% of tightening-cycle observations, or the conditional Rank IC in tightening regimes is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in goods-producing sector activity that precedes downward earnings revisions for cyclical equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, or the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and payroll employment captures a broad-based deterioration in cyclical demand and labor income that is not fully visible in either series alone, and this joint contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of jointly declining HOUST and PAYEMS fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a nested regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate sales and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow positive GDPC1 growth in less than 55% of quarters, or the walk-forward Rank IC of quarterly GDP growth drops below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating nonfarm payroll employment signals healing labor markets and rising household income that drives consumption-led earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns follow accelerating PAYEMS in less than 55% of post-recession recovery months, or the conditional Rank IC in recovery regimes is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.
